title: Bartolomeo Raises His Barrier
description: Pages 1–5 focus on bartolomeo raises his barrier, keeping the Dressrosa conflict centered on the active characters in this part.
scene: This part follows Gladius, Bartolomeo, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5 as zoro, Bartolomeo, Robin, Rebecca, and the others advance through the plateau while Doflamingo's officers try to stop them.

dialogue.speaker: Gladius
dialogue.text: It's those rupture bullets from earlier, ain't it?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: You're facing the wrong way. Luffy's over here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: These are gonna come in handy!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: No, no. Thank you! Thank you for being born. Thank you for existing!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: With this it's straight to level 4!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'll hold them off! Hurry, straw hat!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: My beloved horse lies fallen on this floor...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: I can't go on without giving these bastards retribution! Go!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #21
dialogue.text: The hell happened to your bar- Riers!?

dialogue.speaker: Bartolomeo
dialogue.text: There's a limit... on the total surface area of barrier I can put out at any given time...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #23
dialogue.text: Right now I've used it all on these stairs...

dialogue.speaker: Bartolomeo
dialogue.text: If I can be of use to Luffy-Senpai then I shall gladly give up even this life!!

dialogue.speaker: Bartolomeo
dialogue.text: Until you can use your barriers again I'll fight alongside you! So, keep it together!

summary: Bartolomeo Raises His Barrier develops the chapter's Dressrosa conflict through Gladius, Bartolomeo, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, while preserving the OCR dialogue and keeping unclear speakers readable.
